name: FhirR4BaseSilentIgnore
title: FHIR R4 — Unknown search parameter must be rejected
description: A spec-compliant server must NOT silently drop unknown search parameters. The FHIR R4 specification (search.html#errors) requires the server to return an OperationOutcome describing the error. Some servers respond 200 OK with an unfiltered result set, which is the most dangerous form of search-behavior divergence: the client believes its filter applied.

relatedArtifact.label: FHIR R4 — handling search errors / unknown parameters

purpose: Detects the silent-ignore footgun. mock.health/blog/fhir-server-compare documents this as the headline finding across 7 servers.

test.name: Server rejects unknown search parameter on Patient
test.description: GET Patient?this-is-not-a-real-param=garbage&_count=1 with a deliberately misspelled parameter. A spec-compliant server returns a 4xx with an OperationOutcome. Servers that return 200 are silently filtering — the failure mode this test exists to catch.

assert.label: responseCode is 4xx
assert.description: Spec-compliant rejection of unknown search parameter (any 4xx).
